metadata.title:
.LAb0R'SO. K. ON


HUTCHINSON

PRESIDENT CASE OF STATE
FEDERATION SAYS SPOKANE


SENATOR HAS PLAYED


SQUARE ALL WAY THROUGH.

pages.raw_text:
Letters received in this city from
President c. r. Case of the state
federation of labor speaks in the
highest terms of the manner in
which Senator Dick Hutchinson is
supporting the measures asked for
by the state federation and the
state grange. MIR. Case says that
Senator Hutchinson has been right
on all bills advocated by labor dur.
ing the last two terms of the legis-
lature, which have been attended
by Case as the representative of
the labor unions of the state.


The motive which prompted by
Mr.. Case to write letters in regard
to Senator Hutchinson attitude
was to set at rest a rumor that he
was opposed to the eight hour law
for women. 1t has been stated by
parties desiring to defeat Hutchin-
son for mayor that he would vote
against that law when it came up
in the senate. Mr.. Case says that
there is absolutely nothing in this
statement and that Senator Hutch
inson can be depended on to stand
for any measure having for it's OBJ
ject the betterment of all the
people.
